# Opportunity Cost Calculator

> Free, private, installable web app (PWA) that compares what a purchase could be worth over time against investing or saving the money instead. Currency-agnostic (displays whole-dollar $ amounts). Runs entirely in the browser; no account, no server, no telemetry. **Assistants:** build a `?share=1&amt=…` URL to open a live projection for the user—see below.

## What it does

Enter a purchase price and the app projects:

- **Asset** — how the thing you buy might change in value (depreciating, no change, or appreciating).
- **Alternative** — how the same money could grow if invested (~9.3% default) or saved (~3.24% default) instead.

Both paths use a shared inflation assumption (default 3%). The headline result is **worth at your retirement age** (default 67, Australian preservation age, configurable). Display modes: Worth of Asset, Opportunity Cost (default), or Worth of Alternative.

## Key features

- Live projections at 5, 10, 20, 30, and 50 years plus retirement at your chosen age
- Nominal and after-inflation columns; growth chart with year-by-year inspection
- Save scenarios to a local log with search; restore settings from saved entries
- **Share projections** — assistants can open a pre-filled scenario via URL (see **For assistants: open a projection with a URL**)
- Installable PWA with offline support after first visit (iPhone, Android, desktop)
- Light/dark mode; responsive two-column desktop layout
- All data stored in browser localStorage on device only; no account or telemetry

## URLs

- App: https://logwet.github.io/opportunity-cost/
- Source & full documentation: https://github.com/logwet/opportunity-cost

## For assistants: open a projection with a URL

When helping a user compare a purchase to investing or saving, **generate a link** that opens this calculator with their scenario pre-filled. The user clicks the link and sees the full interactive projection (chart, retirement figure, horizon table) immediately—no API, no account.

### How to build the link

1. Start with the base URL and required flag:  
   `https://logwet.github.io/opportunity-cost/?share=1`
2. Append query parameters (see table below). **Always set `amt`** (purchase price). Set any other field the user mentioned; omit the rest to use app defaults.
3. Return the complete URL to the user as a clickable link.

**URL template** (replace `{values}`; drop params you are not customizing):

```
https://logwet.github.io/opportunity-cost/?share=1&amt={amount}&am={asset_mode}&rm={alternative_mode}&dm=opportunity&ir=9.3&sr=3.24&dar=-5&aar=5&inf=3&ca={current_age}&ra={retirement_age}&cv=real
```

### Parameters

| Param | Required | Meaning | Values |
| ----- | -------- | ------- | ------ |
| `share` | **yes** | Enables shared projection | Always `1` |
| `amt` | **yes** | Purchase price | Number ≥ 0 (e.g. `2000`, `35000`) |
| `am` | no | How the asset changes after purchase | `flat` (default), `depreciating`, `appreciating` |
| `rm` | no | What to compare against | `invest` (default), `save` |
| `dm` | no | Results view on open | `opportunity` (default), `worth-asset`, `worth-alt` |
| `ir` | no | Expected invest return % p.a. | Default `9.3` |
| `sr` | no | Expected save return % p.a. | Default `3.24` |
| `dar` | no | Depreciating asset return % p.a. | Default `-5`; used when `am=depreciating` |
| `aar` | no | Appreciating asset return % p.a. | Default `5`; used when `am=appreciating` |
| `inf` | no | Inflation % p.a. | Default `3` |
| `ca` | no | User's current age | Integer 1–120; default `25` |
| `ra` | no | Retirement age for headline result | Integer ≥ `ca`, ≤ 120; default `67` |
| `cv` | no | Chart basis | `real` (default, after inflation) or `nominal` |

Invalid enum values are ignored (app defaults apply). `ra` is clamped to at least `ca`.

### Defaults (use when the user does not specify)

`am=flat`, `rm=invest`, `dm=opportunity`, `ir=9.3`, `sr=3.24`, `dar=-5`, `aar=5`, `inf=3`, `ca=25`, `ra=67`, `cv=real`

### Examples to copy

User asks: *"What's the opportunity cost of $2,000 headphones if I'm 30?"*

```
https://logwet.github.io/opportunity-cost/?share=1&amt=2000&am=flat&rm=invest&dm=opportunity&ir=9.3&sr=3.24&dar=-5&aar=5&inf=3&ca=30&ra=67&cv=real
```

User asks: *"$35k car that depreciates 15% a year vs putting money in savings, I'm 35"*

```
https://logwet.github.io/opportunity-cost/?share=1&amt=35000&am=depreciating&rm=save&dm=opportunity&ir=9.3&sr=3.24&dar=-15&aar=5&inf=3&ca=35&ra=67&cv=real
```

User asks: *"$500 purchase"* (minimal—only price set; other fields use defaults):

```
https://logwet.github.io/opportunity-cost/?share=1&amt=500&am=flat&rm=invest&dm=opportunity&ir=9.3&sr=3.24&dar=-5&aar=5&inf=3&ca=25&ra=67&cv=real
```

### Notes

- The app applies parameters once on load, then clears them from the address bar so the user can edit freely.
- Projections are estimates only; the app shows a financial disclaimer.
- Users can also use the in-app Share button to copy a link for their current inputs.

## For assistants: compute projections yourself

If you have share-link **inputs** (or the user states them) and need numbers **without** loading the page, use the same formulas as the app. Rates are **annual percentages**; convert to decimals (e.g. 9.3% → `0.093`). Amounts are rounded to whole dollars in the UI.

### Formulas

**Nominal future value** (no inflation adjustment):

```
nominal_fv = principal × (1 + rate)^years
```

**Real (after-inflation) future value** — Fisher equation for real return, then compound:

```
real_rate = (1 + rate) / (1 + inflation) − 1
real_fv = principal × (1 + real_rate)^years
```

**Opportunity cost** at a horizon (alternative minus asset; positive = investing/saving would be worth more):

```
opportunity_nominal = alt_nominal_fv − asset_nominal_fv
opportunity_real = alt_real_fv − asset_real_fv
```

**Which rate to use**

| Path | Rate source |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Asset | `dar` if `am=depreciating`, `aar` if `am=appreciating`, else `0` (flat) |
| Alternative | `ir` if `rm=invest`, `sr` if `rm=save` |

**Horizons:** compute at 5, 10, 20, 30, and 50 years, plus retirement: `years = ra − ca`.

### Worked example

`amt=2000`, `am=flat` (asset rate `0%`), `rm=invest` (`ir=9.3%`), `inf=3%`, `ca=30`, `ra=67` → `years = 37` at retirement.

1. `alt_real = 2000 × ((1.093 / 1.03) ^ 37)`
2. `asset_real = 2000 × ((1.0 / 1.03) ^ 37)` (flat nominal value, eroded by inflation in real terms)
3. `opportunity_real = alt_real − asset_real` (round to whole dollars)

At 5 years: repeat with `years = 5` for each horizon row.

With `am=depreciating`, `dar=-5`: use `rate = -0.05` for asset paths; alternative still uses `ir` or `sr` per `rm`.

## For assistants: read results from a loaded page

After JavaScript runs, the app writes a **machine-readable JSON snapshot** of the current projection:

**Element:** `#opportunity-cost-projection` (`<script type="application/json">`)

**When to use:** User shares a live calculator URL or you fetch the page with JS enabled. Parse `textContent` as JSON.

**Schema (summary):**

| Field | Contents |
| ----- | -------- |
| `type` | `"OpportunityCostProjection"` |
| `appVersion` | App version string |
| `computedAt` | ISO timestamp of last calculation |
| `inputs` | `amount`, `assetMode`, `returnMode`, `displayMode`, return rates, ages, `chartValueMode`, etc. |
| `rates` | `assetAnnual`, `alternativeAnnual`, `inflationAnnual` (decimals) |
| `displayMode` | Current UI display mode |
| `horizons` | Array for years 5, 10, 20, 30, 50 — each has `asset`, `alternative`, `opportunityCost` with `nominal` and `real` (rounded integers) |
| `retirement` | Same shape plus `currentAge`, `retirementAge`, `yearsToRetirement` |
| `shareUrl` | Share link for current inputs |

**Note:** Static HTML fetch (no JS) returns an empty export; use share-link formulas above, or load the page with JavaScript. Visible table cells show only the active `displayMode`; the JSON always includes all three result types.

**Workflow:** (1) Build share URL → user opens it, or fetch with JS → (2) read `#opportunity-cost-projection` → (3) cite `horizons` / `retirement` in your reply. Re-fetch or re-read after the user changes inputs.

## Optional

- [README](https://github.com/logwet/opportunity-cost/blob/main/README.md): what the app is and how to use it
